responses.text: The article from Distractify covers recent instances of the "Blackout Challenge," a dangerous viral trend on TikTok encouraging users to hold their breath or asphyxiate until passing out.
The article focuses on two specific tragedies: the death of a 10-year-old girl in Italy on January 21, 2021, and the hospitalization of a 12-year-old boy in Colorado in March 2021.
Both incidents align precisely with independent reporting.
In January 2021, an Italian 10-year-old girl was found unconscious with her cellphone with a belt tied around her neck by her sibling in Palermo after trying a blackout challenge on TikTok.
Following her death, the Italian Data Protection Authority temporarily blocked access to TikTok for users who could not definitively prove their age was over 13.
On March 22, 2021, 12-year-old Joshua Haileyesus from Aurora, Colorado, was found unconscious in his home, heavily injured after his family suspected he had attempted the challenge using a shoelace.
He spent 19 days on life support before passing away in April 2021.
The claims made by Distractify accurately reflect these two events, which prompted widespread warnings to parents about the dangers of asphyxiation challenges on social media platforms.
